title: hoya capotoanensis subsp. capotoanensis
intro: Hoya capotoanensis subsp. capotoanensis, also known as the Capoatan waxy or Capoatan hoya, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Hoya capotoanensis subsp. capotoanensis include its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion, or as a topical application, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
